title: TAPPaaS Module Field Definitions
description: Defines the structure and valid values for module configuration JSON files (e.g., myModule.json)

description.description: Descriptive text that appears on the Proxmox summary page for the VM
description.example: Nextcloud file sharing and collaboration platform

version.description: Version of the module (not the TAPPaaS system). Used for tracking running versions and determining if upgrades exist.
version.note: Major < 1 indicates module is not production-ready

appVersion.description: Upstream application version deployed by this module. Optional but recommended. Distinct from 'version' (module semver). Treated as an informational string — no format enforced.
appVersion.note: Use the upstream project's own version string: semver, date-based, or tag (e.g. '33.0.3', '1.85.0', 'nightly')

releaseDate.description: Date when this version of the module was released
releaseDate.note: Automatically set to today's date by copy-update-json.sh if not present in source JSON

installTime.description: Timestamp when the module was installed, automatically set by copy-update-json.sh
installTime.note: Automatically set during install. Format: YYYYMMDD-HH:MM:SS (local time)

updateTime.description: Timestamp when the module was last updated, automatically set by update-module.sh
updateTime.note: Automatically set on each update. Format: YYYYMMDD-HH:MM:SS (local time)

variant.description: Variant name when this module was installed as a variant (e.g., 'staging'). Persisted by copy-update-json.sh so update-module.sh can resolve the source module file. Not present for vanilla installs.
variant.note: Set automatically when --variant <name> is used at install time (#207). Treated as an auto-managed field by the 3-way merge — never adopted from the release source.

maintainer.note: Typically a GitHub username prefixed with @

values.Development: Module is under active development, not ready for production
values.Testing: Module is being tested, may have issues
values.Production: Module is stable and ready for production use
values.archived: Module's VM was removed via 'delete-module.sh --archive' but its config and PBS backups are kept for restore (issue #215). Set automatically on archive; inspect-cluster.sh shows it as [archived] rather than NOT RUNNING.
values.external: VM/guest that exists on the cluster but is managed OUTSIDE TAPPaaS (e.g. pfsense, hassosova). A minimal config ({vmid, vmname, status:external, description}) registers it as known-but-unmanaged: no install/update/test/delete lifecycle applies, backup:vm ignores it unless explicitly added, and inspect-cluster.sh shows it as [external] rather than NOT IN CONFIG. Mirrors zones.json state=Manual (issue #216).

location.description: Absolute path to the module directory containing install.sh, update.sh, etc.
location.note: Automatically set by copy-update-json.sh based on the directory where the module JSON resides

vmname.description: Name of the VM, also used as module name and OS hostname

vmid.description: Unique VM ID across all TAPPaaS nodes

vmtag.description: Proxmox tags for the VM. Comma-separated list, no spaces.

node.description: Name of the Proxmox/TAPPaaS node where the module should be installed. If null, defaults to the first node in configuration.json.

values.seabios: Legacy BIOS (for older OSes or special cases)

ostype.description: Proxmox guest OS profile. Controls QEMU clock, ACPI, and balloon-driver behaviour — it does NOT install the OS. The letter in 'l26' is a lowercase L (Linux), not the digit 1.
ostype.note: Pick the value that matches your guest OS. Wrong ostype causes clock drift or incorrect power-management behaviour but does not prevent the VM from booting.

values.l26: Linux kernel 2.6 and later — NixOS, Debian, Ubuntu, and any modern Linux distro. Enables KVM paravirtual clock, assumes UTC hardware clock. USE THIS for all TAPPaaS NixOS and Debian/Ubuntu VMs.
values.win11: Windows 11 / Windows Server 2022 / Windows Server 2025. Enables Windows-specific ACPI, local-time hardware clock, Hyper-V enlightenments, and TPM 2.0 support. USE THIS for all Windows Server 2025 VMs.
values.win10: Windows 10 / Windows Server 2016 / Windows Server 2019. Same Windows treatment as win11 but with an earlier ACPI profile.
values.win2k25: Explicit alias for Windows Server 2025. Equivalent to win11 in Proxmox — win11 is preferred.
values.win2k22: Explicit alias for Windows Server 2022. Equivalent to win11 in Proxmox — win11 is preferred.
values.win2k19: Explicit alias for Windows Server 2019. Equivalent to win10 in Proxmox — win10 is preferred.
values.other: Generic / unknown OS — minimal hypervisor optimisation.

cputype.description: CPU type emulation for the VM
cputype.note: Use 'host' for best performance, or a specific CPU model for migration compatibility

cores.description: Number of CPU cores allocated to the VM

storage.description: Name of the storage pool for the module
storage.note: Storage pool must exist on the target node

pbsStorageName.description: Name of the Proxmox Backup Server datastore / Proxmox storage used for backups. Set on the backup module; read by backup:vm scripts (install/test/restore/backup-manage).
pbsStorageName.note: Allows environments whose PBS storage uses a different name (e.g. 'pbs') to override the default. Used as both the PBS datastore name and the Proxmox storage name.

alwaysBackup.description: Set only on the backup module. List of module names whose VMs are always added to the PBS backup job, even though they do not (and cannot) declare dependsOn backup:vm — typically foundation VMs that bootstrap before the backup server (firewall, tappaas-cicd).
alwaysBackup.note: Each entry is a module/VM name resolved to its VMID via /home/tappaas/config/<name>.json. Registered when the backup module installs/updates.

imageType.description: How the guest's image/rootfs is sourced. Applies to both VMs (cluster:vm) and containers (cluster:lxc); whether the guest is a VM or an LXC is determined by dependsOn (cluster:vm vs cluster:lxc), not by this field.

clone.description: cluster:vm — VM cloned from an existing Proxmox VM template. cluster:lxc — container created (pct create) from a CT template in local:vztmpl, downloaded via pveam if absent.
clone.image_field: cluster:vm: VMID of the template to clone. cluster:lxc: CT template filename (e.g. debian-12-standard_12.12-1_amd64.tar.zst)

iso.description: VM is created with a CD-ROM drive attached to the ISO. ISO is downloaded and placed in local:iso. (VM only.)

img.description: VM disk imports the image (downloaded and unzipped if compressed). Image is discarded after import. (VM only.)

apt.description: Package is a simple apt package to be installed
apt.image_field: Name of the apt package(s) to install

image.description: Image identifier. Interpretation depends on imageType.

by_imageType.clone: cluster:vm — VMID of the Proxmox template to clone. cluster:lxc — CT template filename (downloaded via pveam if absent)
by_imageType.apt: Name of apt package(s) to install

imageLocation.description: URL or local path where the image file is located. For images already on the Proxmox node use the local directory path (e.g. '/var/lib/vz/template/iso/'). For remote images use an https:// URL.
imageLocation.note: For apt, if present, names an additional package repository to add

os.description: Operating system family of the VM. Drives OS-specific cloud-init bootstrapping (e.g. attaching the Debian vendor-data snippet that pre-installs qemu-guest-agent).
os.default: <auto-detected from image filename: debian-*, ubuntu-*, *nixos* -> matching value; else unknown>

values.debian: Debian-family cloud image (apt). Receives the tappaas-debian vendor-data snippet.
values.ubuntu: Ubuntu-family cloud image (apt). Receives the tappaas-debian vendor-data snippet.
values.nixos: NixOS template VM. No vendor-data snippet.
values.windows: Windows Server clone VM. Create-TAPPaaS-VM.sh builds and attaches a per-VM OOBE answer ISO. No cloud-init.
values.unknown: OS family is unknown. No vendor-data snippet.

os.note: Optional. If omitted, Create-TAPPaaS-VM.sh falls back to sniffing the 'image' filename. Set explicitly to override the sniff.

windows.description: Windows-specific configuration options for Windows Server clone VMs.

enableRDP.description: Enable Remote Desktop Protocol. SSH over OpenSSH is always the primary access method.

cloudInit.description: Whether the VM supports cloud-init for initial configuration

values.true: VM supports cloud-init (SSH keys, hostname, etc.)

autoInstall.description: Whether this template can be built fully automatically without operator input. Used by cluster:vm/install-service.sh when a required template is missing — if true, the template is auto-built before the clone proceeds; if false, the operator must build it manually first.

values.true: Fully unattended build — no console interaction required (e.g. tappaas-winserver with autounattend.xml)
values.false: Requires manual installation — operator must run install-module.sh and complete the setup (e.g. tappaas-nixos graphical installer)

sshAccess.description: Whether TAPPaaS can manage this guest over SSH. Set false for sealed appliance images that expose no login shell (e.g. Home Assistant OS). When false, cluster:vm test-service skips its SSH-based health checks (connectivity, disk, memory) and relies on the module's own test.sh for app-level health — so a working appliance is not falsely reported as failing (which would otherwise abort updates).

values.true: TAPPaaS manages the guest over SSH (NixOS VMs, OPNsense as root)
values.false: Unmanaged appliance — no SSH; skip SSH-based cluster:vm tests

HANode.description: Secondary node for High Availability. Defaults to the first node in configuration.json that differs from 'node'. Must be different from 'node' and have the same storage defined. HA is only active when 'cluster:ha' is in dependsOn.

replicationSchedule.description: Cron-style schedule for HA replication interval

bridge0.description: Proxmox bridge for the VM's first network interface (net0)

mac0.description: MAC address for the net0 network port

zone0.description: Security zone for net0. Must exist in zones.json.
zone0.note: Zone determines VLAN tag. 'mgmt' is untagged traffic. camelCase only — no underscores or hyphens (#278).

trunks0.description: Semicolon-separated list of additional zone names to trunk on net0. Zone names are resolved to VLAN tags. Used when a VM needs to see traffic from multiple VLANs on a single NIC (e.g., the firewall).
trunks0.note: Zone names must exist in zones.json, camelCase only [a-zA-Z0-9]. Semicolon-delimited, no spaces. Sentinels: ALL/* expand to every Active zone; NONE = empty.

bridge1.description: Proxmox bridge for the VM's second network interface (net1). If not present, only one NIC is configured.
bridge1.note: Only used if a second network interface is needed

mac1.description: MAC address for the net1 network port

zone1.description: Security zone for net1. Must exist in zones.json.
zone1.note: Only used if bridge1 is defined. Hyphens are NOT allowed — use underscores (#237).

trunks1.description: Semicolon-separated list of additional zone names to trunk on net1. Same format as trunks0.
trunks1.note: Only used if bridge1 is defined. Zone names must exist in zones.json, camelCase only [a-zA-Z0-9].

firewallType.description: Type of firewall in use. Set to 'NONE' when the TAPPaaS OPNsense firewall is not deployed (e.g. using pfSense, UniFi, Cisco, or no firewall).

values.NONE: No TAPPaaS firewall — manual reverse proxy and firewall rule configuration required

firewallType.note: When set to 'NONE', firewall:proxy prints manual configuration instructions instead of calling caddy-manager

aliasType.description: OPNsense alias type for the module's firewall alias (tappaas_module_<vmname>). 'host' (default) targets the FQDN <vmname>.<zone0>.internal resolved via Unbound/dnsmasq. 'network' targets the entire zone0 subnet from zones.json — use for modules representing multiple devices with no single resolvable hostname (e.g. an IoT speaker fleet, a set of physical appliances).

values.network: Network alias → zone0 subnet CIDR from zones.json (multi-device modules)

aliasType.note: When 'network', the alias content is derived from the zone0 subnet — no separate field is needed. The module's zone0 must define an 'ip' (subnet) in zones.json.

ip.description: Static IPv4 address of the device this module represents. Used by firewall:dns to create the DNS host override <vmname>.<zone0>.internal -> <ip>. Must be a static reservation OUTSIDE the zone's DHCP pool; firewall:dns install-service warns (does not fail) if the IP falls inside a configured pool. No default — supply it in the module JSON (typically under config."firewall:dns".ip) or override at install time with --ip <addr>.
ip.note: Hardware modules (no VM) declare this so their DNS entry is provisioned automatically at install time (issue #251). The matching static DHCP reservation (MAC-based) remains a manual prerequisite.

proxyDomain.description: Public domain name for the reverse proxy. Caddy obtains a TLS certificate for this domain.
proxyDomain.note: If not set, defaults to <vmname>.<domain> from configuration.json

proxyPort.description: Target port on the module VM that the reverse proxy forwards traffic to
proxyPort.note: The port the service listens on inside the VM

proxyUpstreamTls.description: Reverse-proxy to an HTTPS upstream instead of plain HTTP. Set true for backends that only speak TLS — e.g. the OPNsense GUI on :8443. firewall:proxy renders the Caddy upstream as https:// with upstream certificate verification skipped (internal/self-signed backends).

values.true: Upstream is HTTPS (skip upstream cert verification)

proxyUpstreamHttp1.description: Force HTTP/1.1 to the upstream (os-caddy HttpVersion=http1). Required for apps whose UI rides a WebSocket behind a TLS upstream — e.g. the UniFi OS console. Without it, Caddy negotiates HTTP/2 with the upstream, which cannot carry a WebSocket Upgrade and returns 500, so the SPA renders blank (issue #339).

values.true: Force HTTP/1.1 to the upstream (WebSocket support)

proxyPreserveHost.description: Force the upstream Host header to the public domain (Caddy header_up Host <domain>). Needed for apps that validate a WebSocket's Origin against the Host header — e.g. the UniFi OS console: Caddy otherwise sends the upstream's own hostname, so the browser's Origin (the public domain) ≠ Host and the WebSocket upgrade returns 500, leaving the SPA blank after login (issue #339). Usually paired with proxyUpstreamHttp1 for WebSocket apps behind a TLS upstream.

proxyTls.description: How firewall:proxy obtains the public TLS certificate for this domain. 'dns01' (default) binds the TAPPaaS-wide wildcard certificate issued by os-acme-client (see acme-setup.sh, issue #254) to this domain via Caddy's per-domain CustomCertificate — no inbound HTTP-01 validation, no per-module DNS API call (one wildcard serves every dns01 module). 'http01' uses classic ACME HTTP-01: Caddy obtains a per-domain cert via the :80 challenge, so the domain MUST be reachable from the internet on port 80 — useful for genuinely public services where DNS-API access is not available. For dns01 to deliver a certificate, the wildcard must already be in OPNsense Trust (run acme-setup.sh on the cicd at INSTALL.md §2.3); until then the module's LAN endpoint still works but the public HTTPS endpoint has no cert.

values.dns01: Wildcard certificate via os-acme-client; bound by refid through Caddy CustomCertificate (no per-module ACME, works for internal-only services)
values.http01: Per-domain ACME HTTP-01 via Caddy itself (no DNS-API needed, but the domain must be reachable from the internet on :80)

proxyAllowedZones.description: Zones (and the literal 'internet') permitted to reach this service through the reverse proxy (issue #206). firewall:proxy compiles this into an os-caddy access list (allow-list by client subnet) attached to the handler; non-matching clients get HTTP 403.
proxyAllowedZones.default: <internal default: every Active 'Service' zone plus home, work and mgmt — NOT the internet>
proxyAllowedZones.note: Zero-trust by default: when omitted, a service is reachable only from the internal trusted zones, never the internet. Add 'internet' to publish it publicly (no restriction). Zone names are resolved to subnets via zones.json. Changing this re-applies on the next install/update of the module.

discoveryMdns.description: Consumer zones to bridge with zone0 in the mDNS repeater (os-mdns-repeater). zone0 is always included as the provider zone.
discoveryMdns.note: Requires firewall:discovery in dependsOn. Idempotently unions zone0 + declared consumer zones into the repeater interface list. Boolean true is deprecated.

discoveryUdpRelay.description: UDP broadcast relay instances via os-udpbroadcastrelay. Each entry creates one relay identified by 'tappaas:<module>:<port>'.

port.description: UDP port to relay (e.g. 36549 for Alfen Nucleus discovery).

zones.description: Zone names whose interfaces receive the relayed broadcast.

discoveryUdpRelay.note: Requires firewall:discovery in dependsOn. Idempotent: checks description before adding. Reload is triggered only when new entries are added.

ports.description: Network ports this module exposes for inbound traffic. Source of truth for ingress validation.

port.description: TCP/UDP port number, or port range as a string (e.g. '8000:8010').

ports.note: Ingress entries must reference ports declared here. Activated when firewall:rules is in dependsOn.

ingress.description: Inbound traffic permitted to this module's ports. Each entry is compiled into an OPNsense pass rule and validated against the destination zone's pinhole-allowed-from policy at compile time.

from.description: Traffic source. One of: zone name (in zones.json), another module name (resolved to an OPNsense host alias for the module's FQDN), the literal 'internet', or 'alias:<name>' to reference a module-local or global alias.

ports.description: Destination ports allowed for this source. Each value must also appear in module.ports[].

description.description: Audit rationale for permitting this traffic. Required — recorded in the OPNsense rule for traceability.

example.description: Caddy reverse proxy forwards the public hostname

ingress.note: Module peer (from = another module name): compiler ensures an OPNsense host alias 'tappaas_module_<peer>' exists, populated with the peer's FQDN; the rule references the alias so DHCP IP changes are absorbed by Unbound resolution. (Underscores: OPNsense alias names cannot contain hyphens.)

egress.description: Outbound traffic exceptions beyond what the source zone's access-to already permits. Compiled into OPNsense pass rules on the source-zone interface.

to.description: Traffic destination. One of: zone name (in zones.json), another module name (resolved to an OPNsense host alias for the module's FQDN), the literal 'internet', or 'alias:<name>'.

description.description: Audit rationale for permitting this egress. Required.

egress.note: Module peer (to = another module name): compiler creates a host alias 'tappaas_module_<peer>' from the peer's FQDN. A warning is emitted when 'to' is a zone not in the source zone's access-to list.

aliases.description: Module-local OPNsense aliases that this module's ingress/egress rules reference via 'alias:<name>'. Module-local aliases override identically named entries in firewall/aliases.json (a warning is emitted on collision).

values.host: Host alias — IPs or FQDNs (FQDN entries are periodically resolved by Unbound)
values.port: Port alias — single ports or ranges
values.url: URL alias — table populated by fetching a URL

addresses.description: Alias contents (IPs, CIDRs, FQDNs, ports, or URLs depending on type).

description.description: Audit rationale for this alias. Required.

llm_cloud_providers.description: Curated whitelist of approved upstream model providers

aliases.note: Aliases are created in OPNsense before referencing rules are applied, and removed on remove-rules unless still referenced by other modules.

natRules.description: Destination-NAT (port-forward) mappings that expose an internal service port on the firewall's WAN interface. Each entry is compiled into an OPNsense rdr-pass rule (translate + allow in one rule). Use this for non-HTTP ports (e.g. SSH); use firewall:proxy for HTTP(S). All rules forward to this module's single target (its 'ip', or <vmname>.<zone0>.internal).

externalPort.description: Port exposed on the firewall WAN interface (e.g. 2022).

internalPort.description: Service port on the target host. Defaults to externalPort when omitted.

description.description: Human-readable purpose of this port-forward (audit/documentation). Combined with the module name to form the rule's identity.

natRules.note: Activated when firewall:nat is in dependsOn. Rules are created as rdr-pass on the WAN interface, so no separate firewall:rules ingress entry is needed for the forwarded port.

dependsOn.description: List of services this module depends on. Each entry is a 'module:service' pair identifying which module delivers the service.
dependsOn.note: The installer validates that each referenced module is installed and provides the named service

provides.description: List of services this module provides. Service names only (the module is implicit from the JSON filename).
provides.note: Each listed service must have a corresponding services/<service>/install-service.sh and update-service.sh

config.description: Optional Pattern-A nesting (issue #161, Pattern C hybrid). Groups per-service configuration under the '<module>:<service>' dependency coordinate instead of (or alongside) flat top-level fields. Purely a readability/authoring convenience: TAPPaaS NORMALIZES it to the flat form at deploy (copy-update-json.sh) and at validation (check_json), so all tooling still reads flat top-level fields. A module may be written fully flat (current form), fully Pattern-A, or mixed.

^[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9-]*:[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-.description: Configuration for one declared dependency. The fields inside are ordinary module fields (cores, proxyDomain, …) scoped to that service.

rules: Every key in 'config' must also appear in 'dependsOn' (a config block for an undeclared dependency is an error).
A field must not appear in both the header and a config block, nor in two config blocks (ambiguous after flattening — rejected).
Multi-service header fields (e.g. zone0, vmname, vmid) stay at the top level — they are owned by the module, not a single service.
config.note: Convert between forms with module-format.sh (to-flat | to-config). Backward compatible: flat modules are untouched.

identity.description: OIDC integration contract for a module that dependsOn identity:identity (ADR-006). Consumed by services/identity/install-service.sh. Omit for forward-auth modules (those use identity:accessControl).

providesAdminRole.description: Opt-in: create and bind a <scope>-<module>-admins group so a per-module admin role exists. Default false keeps Authentik uncluttered for modules with no admin concept.

oidcRedirectPaths.description: OIDC callback paths, each appended to https://<proxyDomain> to form a redirect URI. Default suits Nextcloud user_oidc.

scopes.description: OIDC scope-mapping names the provider should expose. Default (empty) yields openid/email/profile; Authentik’s profile scope already emits the groups claim.

secretsEnv.description: Absolute path on the module VM where OIDC_CLIENT_ID/OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET/OIDC_DISCOVERY_URI are written (mode 600). Default /etc/secrets/<base-module>.env.

configureService.description: systemd unit on the module VM to restart after writing the env so the app registers the provider (e.g. nextcloud-configure-oidc.service). Empty = applies on next nixos-rebuild/boot.

module_structure.description: Standard files in a TAPPaaS module directory

install.sh.description: Installation script called with module name as argument

update.sh.description: Update script called periodically to keep module updated

<module>.meta.json.description: Optional, cluster:lxc only. Carries LXC-specific provisioning details that are intentionally NOT validated against this schema (so device/passthrough config stays flexible). Read by cluster:lxc Create-TAPPaaS-LXC.sh. A module declares cluster:lxc (not cluster:vm) in dependsOn; declaring both is rejected by install-module.sh.
<module>.meta.json.required: if cluster:lxc and the container needs passthrough/bind-mounts/non-default lxc options

gpu.description: Optional. GPU device passthrough. When present, the provisioner emits lxc.cgroup2.devices.allow entries and bind-mounts /dev/kfd and the render node into the container.

render_node.description: DRI render node name, e.g. renderD128

bindMounts.description: Optional. Host directories bind-mounted into the container as mpN entries. NOTE: containers with bind mounts cannot use pct snapshot — rollback is unavailable for these modules.

lxcOptions.description: Optional. Overrides for pct create defaults.

unprivileged.description: 0 = privileged (required for GPU passthrough), 1 = unprivileged. Default 1.

services/<service-name>/install-service..description: Script called when a module consuming this service is installed. Called with the consuming module name as argument.

services/<service-name>/update-service.s.description: Script called when a module consuming this service is updated. Called with the consuming module name as argument.

services/<service-name>/pinhole.json.description: Declares the network ports a service exposes. When a consumer module depends on this service across zones, firewall:rules synthesises an auto-pinhole rule per declared port (subject to the destination zone's pinhole-allowed-from policy). Issue #173.

ports.description: Network ports this service exposes for consumers in other zones.

port.description: TCP/UDP port number, or port range as a string (e.g. '8000:8010').

naming_conventions.module_name: Typically the main software product or capability being delivered
naming_conventions.vm_name: Same as module name by default
naming_conventions.hostname: Same as module name by default
